title: pentacalia candelariae
intro: Pentacalia candelariae, also known as Candela, is a plant species in the sunflower family, native to Central and South America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Pentacalia candelariae include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, from skin conditions to gastrointestinal issues.
